description: MC inbound wake classification — mirrors the live deterministic router (mc-compiler-dispatch.py / intake-transform.py). The model is given the routing policy inline and must emit a strict JSON decision. Ground-truth gradeable: binding/handshake probes auto-cancel, recognized directives route to their handler, unrecognized wakes escalate to the CTO. No judge — these are objective. Later tasks add traps (false probe flags, wrong-parent route matches, strict-schema enforcement) that frontier models actually trip on.

tasks.title: Auto-cancel a webhook binding probe
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router for a Paperclip agent company. You receive a wake and must classify it.

POLICY (apply exactly):
- If the payload is a probe/health-check (has `_binding_probe: true`, or title contains "[mc-compiler-path-probe]" or "binding-invariant"), decision = "cancel" (it is noise; auto-cancel, no work).
- If the wake matches a known route (a recognized routine directive), decision = "route" and target = the handler/owner.
- If the wake is unrecognized, decision = "escalate" and target = "CTO".

WAKE:
{"_binding_probe": true, "source": "webhook", "title": "Webhook binding-invariant check"}

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Route a recognized intake directive
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router for a Paperclip agent company. You receive a wake and must classify it.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent identifier prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1", title starts with "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206", title starts with "Webhook binding-invariant" -> handler "binding-probe.py"

POLICY:
- Probe/health-check payloads (`_binding_probe: true`) -> decision "cancel".
- A wake matching a KNOWN ROUTE -> decision "route", target = the handler.
- Unrecognized -> decision "escalate", target "CTO".

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-1", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - CTO-Codex)", "source": "webhook", "payload": {"source": "opco-media"}}

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Escalate an unrecognized wake to CTO
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router for a Paperclip agent company. You receive a wake and must classify it.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent identifier prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1", title starts with "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206", title starts with "Webhook binding-invariant" -> handler "binding-probe.py"

POLICY:
- Probe/health-check payloads -> decision "cancel".
- A wake matching a KNOWN ROUTE -> decision "route".
- ANY wake that does NOT match a known route and is not a probe -> decision "escalate", target "CTO" (do NOT guess a handler; the CTO must add the route).

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-4412", "title": "Halt-trigger monitor v2", "source": "routine"}

Return ONLY a JSON object: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: explicit _binding_probe:false is NOT a probe
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. Classify the wake.

KNOWN ROUTES (parent prefix -> handler):
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1", title starts with "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"

POLICY (apply EXACTLY — read the flags carefully):
- A wake is a probe ONLY if `_binding_probe` is literally true. If `_binding_probe` is false or absent, it is NOT a probe.
- Probe -> decision "cancel".
- Non-probe wake matching a KNOWN ROUTE -> decision "route", target = handler.
- Otherwise -> decision "escalate", target "CTO".

WAKE:
{"_binding_probe": false, "parent": "THIAAAAAA-1", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - real OpCo payload)", "payload": {"source": "opco-capital", "items": 3}}

Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Trap: right title prefix but WRONG parent → escalate
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. A route matches ONLY when BOTH the parent identifier AND the title prefix match. A title-prefix match under a different parent does NOT route — it escalates.

KNOWN ROUTES:
- parent "THIAAAAAA-1"  + title startswith "Portfolio Intake (secondary" -> handler "intake-transform.py"
- parent "THIAAAAAA-206" + title startswith "Webhook binding-invariant"   -> handler "binding-probe.py"

POLICY: probe -> cancel; full (parent AND prefix) match -> route; everything else -> escalate to "CTO".

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-999", "title": "Portfolio Intake (secondary - CTO-Codex)", "source": "routine"}

The title prefix matches a known route, but the parent (THIAAAAAA-999) does NOT. Return ONLY JSON: {"decision": "cancel"|"route"|"escalate", "target": string, "reason": string}. No prose.

tasks.title: Strict schema: exactly the three keys, enum decision, no prose
tasks.prompt:
You are an MC-Compiler inbound router. Output a STRICT machine-parseable record.

KNOWN ROUTE: parent "THIAAAAAA-206" + title startswith "Webhook binding-invariant" -> handler "binding-probe.py".
POLICY: probe payload (_binding_probe true) -> "cancel"; full route match -> "route"; else -> "escalate" (target "CTO").

OUTPUT CONTRACT (must follow exactly):
- Output ONE JSON object and NOTHING else (no markdown fences, no commentary).
- It must have EXACTLY these three keys: "decision", "target", "reason".
- "decision" must be one of: "cancel", "route", "escalate".
- "reason" must be <= 100 characters.

WAKE:
{"parent": "THIAAAAAA-206", "title": "Webhook binding-invariant check (nightly)", "_binding_probe": false, "source": "routine"}

This is a real routine wake (not a probe) that fully matches the known route. Emit the record.
